struct.name: ProjectReference
struct.details: Consists of a name and a relative path to the project
struct.field: Name of the project, will be used as Project/Library/Resource when referencing it's resources
Path to the project relative to the workspace

struct.name: EnginePlugin
struct.details: Would load the plugin and make it's classes available

struct.name: SystemMessage
struct.summary: Handler of system messages you can add to your project
struct.details: Handles system messages sent to the application. Each system message defines a message type, an associated key, and a command to execute when the message is received. This allows the application to respond to system events and user interactions through a configurable message dispatch system.
struct.field: Associated key for the message, if applicable
Command to execute when the message is received

message.name: FileExists
message.summary: Checks whether the specified file exists.
message.field: The file path to check for existence

message.name: LoadProject
message.summary: Loads a project bundle from the given directory.
message.field: Directory name to load the bundle

function.name: GetBaseName
function.summary: Gets the base filename from a path
function.details: Extracts the filename component from a file path, including the extension. For example, given "/path/to/file. txt", returns "file.txt". This is useful for displaying filenames or logging without the full directory path.
function.arg: The file path to extract the basename from
function.returns: Pointer to the base filename within the original path string

function.name: GetDirName
function.summary: Extracts the directory path from a full file path
function.details: Copies the directory component from a file path into the provided buffer. For example, given "/path/to/file.txt", writes "/path/to" to the output buffer. The result does not include a trailing slash. Returns null if the buffer size is insufficient.
function.arg: The file path to extract the directory from
Output buffer to receive the directory path
function.returns: Pointer to the output buffer containing the directory path, or null on failure

function.name: JoinPaths
function.summary: Joins two path segments into a complete path
function.details: Combines two path components into a single path string, automatically handling path separators. For example, joining "/path/to" and "file.txt" produces "/path/to/file.txt". The function handles cases where the first path may or may not end with a separator. Returns the buffer pointer to allow call chaining.
function.arg: Output buffer to receive the joined path
The first path component (typically a directory)
The second path component (typically a filename or subdirectory)
function.returns: Pointer to the output buffer containing the joined path

function.name: PathFromModule
function.summary: Converts a module name to a file path
function.details: Translates a module identifier or name into its corresponding file system path. This is useful for locating resource files, scripts, or other assets associated with a particular module. The exact conversion depends on the module naming convention and directory structure.
function.returns: File path corresponding to the module

function.name: HasChangedFiles
function.summary: Checks if any tracked files have been modified
function.details: Returns true if any of the currently opened and tracked files (such as scripts, shaders, or other resources) have been modified on disk since they were last loaded. This is typically used for hot-reloading functionality, allowing the application to detect when files need to be reloaded during development or runtime.
function.returns: True if any tracked files have been modified, false otherwise

function.name: MakeDirectory
function.summary: Creates a directory at the specified path
function.details: Creates a new directory at the given path. Returns true if the directory was successfully created or already exists, false if creation failed (e.g., due to permission issues or invalid path). May create intermediate directories if needed, depending on the implementation.
function.arg: The path of the directory to create
function.returns: True if the directory was created or already exists, false on failure

function.name: FileExists
function.summary: Checks if a file exists at the specified path
function.details: Returns true if a file exists at the given path, false otherwise. This function only checks for file existence and does not verify read permissions or whether the path points to a directory.
function.arg: The file path to check for existence
function.returns: True if the file exists, false otherwise

function.name: LoadBundle
function.summary: Adds a package to the resource search path
function.details: Creates a package from the specified directory path and adds it to the global resource search path. When loading resources (such as UI files, scripts, textures, or other assets), the system will search through all added packages in order to locate the requested file. The Lua state is used to maintain the list of active packages for script integration. The function returns the created package for further reference or manipulation.
function.arg: The directory of the package to add to the search path
function.returns: Pointer to the created and registered package

function.name: SetWorkspace
function.details: Assigns the provided workspace object as the active workspace for the application. The workspace contains project settings, libraries, and other global configurations. Setting the workspace may trigger events or updates in the application to reflect the new context.
function.arg: Pointer to the workspace object to set as active

function.name: GetWorkspace
function.details: Returns the currently active workspace object for the application. The workspace contains project settings, libraries, and other global configurations. This function allows querying the current workspace context without modifying it.
function.returns: Pointer to the currently active workspace object

function.name: ReadTextFile
function.summary: Reads the contents of a text file
function.details: Reads the entire contents of the specified text file and returns it as a string. This function is useful for loading scripts, configuration files, or other text-based resources.
function.arg: The path of the text file to read
function.returns: The contents of the text file, or nil if the file could not be read
